reviews.review_msg: Brad is a great person, you can tell. His ability to make the material simple for students is low. Midterms were simple, but the averages were outrageously high, so his final was impossible. Cross-reference marking with someone else-high % of errors from the stats workshop. Difficult class for students without strong stats background.

She is demanding at seems confusing at first but in the end she will reward you if you worked hard. She tries to push you to think outside the box. There is not free marks. She cares if students learned something and if you attend her office hour she will clarify things.

She is demanding at seems confusing at first but in the end she will reward you if you worked hard. She tries to push you to think outside the box. There is not free marks. She cares if students learned something and if you attend her office hour she will clarify things.
One of the best.  A damn smart man.  You'll learn a ton if you take a course but be prepared to work hard.
midterms were almost identical to the practice midterms Final was brutal though(class average was around 40% even with generous partial marks given) Quite a lot of formulas to memorize and understand

Very clear and concise. The value I received from his course was high, not only is his textbook available for free, he also posts all the lecture slides that you need to succeed in his class without any blanks. Bottom line, understand the "big picture" behind the material taught and you will do great. David you are awesome.
A bit tough but very clear.
I think he's over-rated. His exams are easy, but that's about it. Try talking to him during office hours! If he doesn't like you, you won't like it.
A Bad Prof with uneducated manner when talking to students. I rate him 0, even though he is good at teaching!
fair prof, interesting lectures , knows what he is talking about

one of the best at SFU..interesting lectures, very passionate about the subject....however, u gotta know ur calculas...as for the final and the midterm ,, very similar to assignments and other problems done in class
wow. wow. wow. avoid this man. copy his notes. study the book. no no. mermorize the book and notes. do all of problems on the web. go to tutorial. love algerbra and NO economics. you MIGHT get a B
Very unclear notes, unable to explain, but he is nice

She is way better than another instructor who teaches physics aspect of this course. you can't listen in lecture because she keeps people busy writing overhead notes (filling blanks). Term papers!!! not fair marking at all and for 3 credit course i think its too much work to have both presentation and term paper! & tutorials not helpful at all
